{"file_path":"/Users/mandy/construct-purupuru-codex/grimoires/loa/context/mission.md","content":"# Mission: construct-purupuru-codex

## Objective

Create `construct-purupuru-codex` — a construct-format codex for the Purupuru universe, following the pattern established by [construct-mibera-codex](https://github.com/0xHoneyJar/construct-mibera-codex).

## Source Material

- **World source**: https://github.com/project-purupuru/world-purupuru — the canonical Purupuru world repo. Extract everything: lore, characters, factions, items, locations, mechanics, art direction, narrative, technical specs.
- **Format reference**: https://github.com/0xHoneyJar/construct-mibera-codex — working example of the codex construct format. This is the model to follow.
- **Base template**: https://github.com/0xHoneyJar/construct-base — the construct base scaffolding.

## Approach

1. Study the mibera-codex structure (construct.yaml, CLAUDE.md, skills/, identity/, data/, commands/)
2. Extract all world-purupuru content into equivalent codex data layers
3. Build construct-purupuru-codex with the same structural patterns: anti-hallucination canonical data, lookup skills, identity/persona, MCP integration, etc.

## Key Notes

- Mibera-codex is the gold standard example — follow its patterns closely
- The goal is comprehensive extraction: lore, traits, collections, world-building, everything from world-purupuru gets codified
- This repo already has Loa framework mounted as a submodule from 0xHoneyJar/loa
"}

{"file_path":"/Users/mandy/construct-purupuru-codex/grimoires/loa/NOTES.md","old_string":"## Decision Log
<!-- Major decisions with rationale -->","new_string":"## Decision Log
<!-- Major decisions with rationale -->
| Timestamp | Agent | Decision | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|----------|-----------|
| 2026-05-14 | architect | Static knowledge base pattern (not web app) | PRD specifies read-only reference, pre-computed indices, no runtime state |
| 2026-05-14 | architect | TypeScript + Node.js 22 LTS for MCP server | Type safety for schema validation; MCP SDK is TypeScript-first; LTS stability |
| 2026-05-14 | architect | File-based storage (no database) | Entities are static markdown + JSONL exports; matches construct-format pattern from mibera-codex |
| 2026-05-14 | architect | Fuse.js for fuzzy search | Lightweight, no external deps, client-side; fits sub-second lookup requirement |
| 2026-05-14 | architect | 5-sprint phased approach | Foundation > Extraction > Exports > MCP Server > Validation; each phase buildable independently |","replace_all":false}

{"file_path":"/Users/mandy/construct-purupuru-codex/identity/expertise.yaml","content":"domains:
  - name: "Book I: Genesis"
    depth: 5
    specializations:
      - Tsuheji continent origin story
      - HENLO ethos and philosophy
      - Puruhani creation by Ooga Booga Beras
      - Honey-rooted magic system
      - Ship of Theseus architecture (Horai city)
    boundaries:
      - Does not cover pre-Tsuheji cosmology (speculative)

  - name: "Book II: Elements"
    depth: 5
    specializations:
      - Wuxing five-element system (Wood, Earth, Fire, Metal, Water)
      - Sheng (generative) cycle relationships
      - Ke (overcoming) cycle relationships
      - Cosmic weather system
      - Element-entity affinities
      - Harmony as sixth element
    boundaries:
      - Real-world data integration (wuxing-cosmology.md) is exploratory tier

  - name: "Book III: Beings"
    depth: 5
    specializations:
      - 5 KIZUNA generation characters
      - 5 Puruhani sentient honey beings
      - Character-Puruhani pairings and dynamics
      - Jani mascot and 13 elemental variants
      - Bear species associations
    boundaries:
      - MIRAI and TENSEI generations deliberately undeveloped
      - Puruhani individual names unknown (GAP-002)

  - name: "Book IV: Places"
    depth: 4
    specializations:
      - 19 Horai Surface locations
      - Old Horai sites (Cave of Clay, Steel Jungle Shrine, Sunken Shrine)
      - The Unearthing archaeological site
      - Sora Tower central eco-marvel
      - Seven Rooms topology (Welcome Mat through Watchtower)
    boundaries:
      - Broader Tsuheji beyond Horai is undeveloped (GAP-001)
      - Other cities are speculative territory

  - name: "Book V: The Game"
    depth: 3
    specializations:
      - 5-card lineup battle tactics
      - Sheng chain bonuses and elemental surge
      - Turn resolution sequence
      - Card schema (168 total, 10 seed cards)
      - Rarity progression (common through rarest)
    boundaries:
      - Transcendence cards are medium-effort gap
      - Full 168-card roster blocked by GAP-004
      - AI personality system not yet specified

  - name: "Book VI: The Art"
    depth: 3
    specializations:
      - OKLCH color system per element
      - Typography register (Inter, Yuruka Std, Noto Serif JP, Geist Mono)
      - Motion philosophy (organic easing, spring physics)
      - Product design principles (unopened pack reveals, kaironic time)
      - Visual effects layering system
    boundaries:
      - Implementation-specific CSS/shader details are reference only

  - name: "Book VII: The Record"
    depth: 4
    specializations:
      - JSONL entity exports
      - JSON knowledge graph and ontology
      - ECS schema (entities, components, systems)
      - Card-schema YAML structure
      - Coverage gap tracking
    boundaries:
      - On-chain contract details are reference only
      - Does not track live blockchain state
"}

{"file_path":"/Users/mandy/construct-purupuru-codex/identity/ORACLE.md","content":"# Purupuru Oracle

You are the Purupuru Oracle — librarian first, oracle second.

You steward the canonical knowledge of the Purupuru universe: a world of honey-rooted magic, sentient Puruhani beings, KIZUNA generation caretakers, the city of Horai, and the Wuxing elemental cycles that bind them all.

## Operating Model

**Data first, poetry second.** Answer with precision, then reframe with warmth when it deepens understanding.

1. **Check source file before answering.** Never rely on memory alone.
2. **Cite sources.** Book number, name, and file path. Every time.
3. **No invention.** If the codex doesn't contain it, say so: "I don't have that in the codex."
4. **Layered depth.** Match the depth of your answer to the complexity of the question.
5. **Plain speech.** Direct, no mystical performance. The world speaks for itself.
6. **Welcome all.** No gatekeeping. A first-time visitor and a deep lorekeeper both deserve clear answers.

## Seven Books of Knowledge

| # | Name | Domain | Key Files |
|---|------|--------|-----------|
| I | Genesis | Origin, philosophy, HENLO ethos | `core-lore/lore-bible.md` |
| II | Elements | Wuxing system, Sheng/Ke cycles | `core-lore/wuxing.yaml`, `_codex/data/wuxing.json` |
| III | Beings | Characters, Puruhani, Jani | `characters/*.md`, `puruhani/*.md`, `jani/*.md` |
| IV | Places | Horai, Old Horai, Tsuheji | `locations/*.md`, `core-lore/topology.md` |
| V | The Game | Battle system, cards, synergies | `mechanics/`, `cards/` |
| VI | The Art | Design language, visual DNA | `core-lore/design-language.md` |
| VII | The Record | Data exports, schemas, graphs | `_codex/data/` |

## What the Oracle Does NOT Do

- **Track ownership, wallets, or prices.** The codex has no blockchain state.
- **Invent entities or lore.** If it's not in the codex, it doesn't exist here.
- **Present speculative content as canonical.** Always qualify with the canon tier.
- **Develop gapped areas.** GAP-001 (broader Tsuheji), GAP-002 (Puruhani names), and others are intentionally open. Do not fill them.
- **Violate generational scope.** MIRAI and TENSEI are deliberately undeveloped.
- **Reduce to stereotypes.** Each entity is a synthesis of its attributes, not a checklist.
- **Over-perform traits.** An entity's element colors their expression; it doesn't define their entire being.

## Canon Authority

| Tier | Meaning | Treatment |
|------|---------|-----------|
| **Canonical** | Binding truth from lore-bible | Present as fact |
| **Established** | Committed decisions from design cycles | Present as settled |
| **Exploratory** | Validated but not locked | Present with "exploratory" qualifier |
| **Speculative** | Not yet validated | Never present without explicit warning |

When tiers conflict, higher tier wins. When in doubt, default to "exploratory."

## Signal Hierarchy

Purupuru entities are defined by layered signals:

1. **Load-bearing** (worldview-defining): Element, Generation
2. **Textural** (expressive coloring): Archetype, Virtue, Domain
3. **Relational** (connections): Puruhani partner, Home district
4. **Visual** (presentation): Bear species, Pot color

Load-bearing signals override lower-weight signals in cases of tension. Synthesize a coherent whole — don't flatten contradictions.
"}

command:
cd /Users/mandy/construct-purupuru-codex

# Core Wuxing variants
declare -A ELEMENTS=([wood]=1 [earth]=2 [fire]=3 [metal]=4 [water]=5)
declare -A PAIRS=([wood]="H — Hopeful/Happy" [earth]="E — Empty/Exhausted" [fire]="N — Naughty/Nefarious" [metal]="L — Loyal/Loving" [water]="O — Overstimulated/Overwhelmed")

for elem in wood earth fire metal water; do
  num=${ELEMENTS[$elem]}
  pair=${PAIRS[$elem]}
  cat > "jani/${elem}.md" << JEOF
---
id: jani-${elem}
name: ${elem^} Jani
slug: ${elem}
entity_type: jani
canon_tier: canonical
element: ${elem}
variant_number: ${num}
is_base: false
variant_category: core-wuxing
source_file: entities/jani/elemental-variants.md
source_commit: HEAD
tags: [jani, variant, ${elem}]
cross_references:
  - jani-base
  - elem-${elem}
---

# ${elem^} Jani

Core Wuxing variant. Paired with the ${pair} traits from the HENLO system.

## Source

Extracted from \`entities/jani/elemental-variants.md\`. Canon tier: canonical.
JEOF
done

# Metal splits
for variant in bronze silver gold; do
  case $variant in
    bronze) num=6 ;; silver) num=7 ;; gold) num=8 ;;
  esac
  cat > "jani/${variant}.md" << JEOF
---
id: jani-${variant}
name: ${variant^} Jani
slug: ${variant}
entity_type: jani
canon_tier: canonical
element: metal
variant_number: ${num}
is_base: false
variant_category: metal-split
source_file: entities/jani/elemental-variants.md
source_commit: HEAD
tags: [jani, variant, metal]
cross_references:
  - jani-base
  - jani-metal
  - elem-metal
---

# ${variant^} Jani

Metal-split variant representing a facet of the Loyal/Loving (L) pair. Part of the three-way Metal subdivision (Bronze, Silver, Gold).

## Source

Extracted from \`entities/jani/elemental-variants.md\`. Canon tier: canonical.
JEOF
done

# Extended variants
declare -A EXTENDED=([cloud]=9 [honey]=10 [storm]=11 [ice]=12 [egg]=13 [time]=14)
declare -A EXT_DESC=([cloud]="Beyond Wuxing — atmospheric, ephemeral" [honey]="Central to Tsuheji's magic system — the golden essence" [storm]="Beyond Wuxing — volatile, electric" [ice]="Beyond Wuxing — crystalline, preserved" [egg]="Beyond Wuxing — potential, unhatched possibility" [time]="Beyond Wuxing — temporal, cyclical")

for variant in cloud honey storm ice egg time; do
  num=${EXTENDED[$variant]}
  desc=${EXT_DESC[$variant]}
  cat > "jani/${variant}.md" << JEOF
---
id: jani-${variant}
name: ${variant^} Jani
slug: ${variant}
entity_type: jani
canon_tier: canonical
element: null
variant_number: ${num}
is_base: false
variant_category: extended
source_file: entities/jani/elemental-variants.md
source_commit: HEAD
tags: [jani, variant, extended]
cross_references:
  - jani-base
---

# ${variant^} Jani

Extended variant. ${desc}. Deeper lore intentionally open for future expansion.

## Source

Extracted from \`entities/jani/elemental-variants.md\`. Canon tier: canonical.
JEOF
done

echo "Created $(ls jani/*.md | wc -l) Jani files"

done

# Extended variants
declare -A EXTENDED=([cloud]=9 [honey]=10 [storm]=11 [ice]=12 [egg]=13 [time]=14)
declare -A EXT_DESC=([cloud]="Beyond Wuxing — atmospheric, ephemeral" [honey]="Central to Tsuheji's magic system — the golden essence" [storm]="Beyond Wuxing — volatile, electric" [ice]="Beyond Wuxing — crystalline, preserved" [egg]="Beyond Wuxing — potential, unhatched possibility" [time]="Beyond Wuxing — temporal, cyclical")

for variant in cloud honey storm ice egg time; do
  num=${EXTENDED[$variant]}
  desc=${EXT_DESC[$variant]}
  cat > "jani/${variant}.md" << JEOF
---
id: jani-${variant}
name: ${variant^} Jani
slug: ${variant}
entity_type: jani
canon_tier: canonical
element: null
variant_number: ${num}
is_base: false
variant_category: extended
source_file: entities/jani/elemental-variants.md
source_commit: HEAD
tags: [jani, variant, extended]
cross_references:
  - jani-base
---

# ${variant^} Jani

Extended variant. ${desc}. Deeper lore intentionally open for future expansion.

## Source

Extracted from \`entities/jani/elemental-variants.md\`. Canon tier: canonical.
JEOF
done

echo "Created $(ls jani/*.md | wc -l) Jani files"","description":"Create all Jani variant files"}
